name: Assistant Manager

attacks.name: Triple laser wave
attacks.tell: The Assistant Manager raises his arm and activates three laser beams in rapid succession
attacks.howToAvoid: Run in a zigzag or jump between lasers so they don't hit; blocking is impossible
attacks.notes: Each bolt deals moderate energy damage; in phase 2 it rotates them faster

attacks.name: electric field
attacks.tell: Activate 4 pillars of which inflict electrical damage
attacks.howToAvoid: Avoid getting attached to these pillars
attacks.notes: They activate at two times during the fight

attacks.name: Summoning Reinforcements
attacks.tell: Makes an animation of pressing a button on his chest, making a beeping sound
attacks.howToAvoid: N/A (booster will appear). Prepare for new enemies in the area
attacks.notes: Summons additional TAYZ.T and ARC.R robots during the fight. Weaken or remove adds quickly so you don't get overwhelmed

attacks.name: electric shock
attacks.tell: Generates electric waves similar to ARC.R
attacks.howToAvoid: Maintain distance to be able to see the trajectory of the projectile, dodge by walking or bounce it with a shield

bossPhases.label: Phase 1
bossPhases.summary: It opens as a relatively clean duel: the Assistant Manager drops in with two TAYZ.Ts and pressures you with electric shots while trying to force movement.
bossPhases.trigger: Begins as soon as it lands in the Black Anthill Lab arena.
newPatterns: The two starting TAYZ.Ts are already part of the phase and punish anyone who tunnels on the boss.

attacks.name: Quick Shock Shot
attacks.tell: It raises its arm and fires a short electric burst straight ahead.
attacks.howToAvoid: Strafe sideways or perfect block it so you do not give away free damage.
attacks.notes: This is its most common punish when you stay in a straight line.

attacks.name: Charged Shock Shot
attacks.tell: It pauses briefly and charges its arm before releasing a heavier shot.
attacks.howToAvoid: Circle it or cut the angle; the delay is longer, but the damage is too.
attacks.notes: It makes you respect mid-range even when the opening looks safe.

bossPhases.label: Phase 2
bossPhases.summary: The room turns much more hostile: an ARC.R joins and the static-charge pillars power up, so space management starts to matter as much as blocking the boss.
bossPhases.trigger: It enters at 75% health with a shout and the activation of the electric pillars.
newPatterns: An extra ARC.R shoots from the sides while the pillars cut off safe paths.

attacks.name: Static Charge Pillars
attacks.tell: The arena pillars light up and discharge electricity around their bases.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not hug the edges; identify the live pillar and rotate before it traps you.
attacks.notes: It does not win the fight alone, but it breaks any comfortable rhythm if you ignore the room.

attacks.name: Quick Shock Shot
attacks.tell: It keeps using short shots to punish you once the pillars have already displaced you.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not run in a straight line after repositioning; vary your dodge timing.
attacks.notes: This phase kills more through overlap than through any single isolated move.

attacks.name: Charged Shock Shot
attacks.tell: It charges its arm more visibly before releasing a heavier shot.
attacks.howToAvoid: Evade it with lateral space; trying to tank it while the room is already busy is expensive.
attacks.notes: It often comes out when you spend too long reading the pillars.

bossPhases.label: Phase 3
bossPhases.summary: The final phase mixes everything at once: two more TAYZ.Ts, a last ARC.R, live pillars, and the laser pattern that turns the room into a pure reading check.
bossPhases.trigger: It enters at 50% health with more reinforcements and the laser pattern activating.
newPatterns: The minions do not respawn again until the next transition, so cleaning up adds is still valuable.

attacks.name: Triple Laser Wave
attacks.tell: Three beams sweep the room in sequence and force jumps or elevation changes.
attacks.howToAvoid: Keep your eyes on the emitter and do not spend your jump too early.
attacks.notes: This is the pattern that defines the last phase and the reason standing still almost never works.

attacks.name: Static Charge Pillars
attacks.tell: The pillars remain active while the lasers sweep the room.
attacks.howToAvoid: Plan a short route, not a long escape; precision matters more than pure running here.
attacks.notes: The overlap between lasers and pillars is the truly dangerous part of the finish.

attacks.name: Summoning Reinforcements
attacks.tell: The Assistant Manager emits a signal and drops another wave of support robots into the arena.
attacks.howToAvoid: If you have breathing room, kill adds first; otherwise circle the boss until the targets separate.
attacks.notes: The fight collapses once you let the boss and the robots shoot you from different angles.

description: The chief of security in the Black Anthill Lab and the first major story boss. It exists to train grunts and kill intruders.
behavior: It mixes rapid static fire with arena scripts that activate towers, lasers, and electric balls. The fight becomes more chaotic as it adds more support robots and room hazards.
interactionWithPlayer: It is hostile from the moment the encounter starts and will not let the player pass deeper into the lab until defeated.
interactionWithCreatures: It cooperates with TAYZ.T and ARC.R units. Each new phase drops fresh waves of robots into the arena to overwhelm the player.
strategy: Hammer damage plus salty or sour pressure is the cleanest answer. The real priority is deleting minions as soon as they enter and learning the safe zones for lasers and shock pillars before trying to maximize damage.
respawnInfo: Does not respawn. It can only be defeated once per save.

combatStats.attackDamageSummary:
Quick shot: 70
Charged shot: 85
Laser pillar: 30
Shock pillar: 35
